title: Bridge Essay: Understanding Differences in Wayfinding Strategies.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.

**Understanding Differences in Wayfinding Strategies.**. Top Cogn Sci.. PMID: 34973064. DOI: 10.1111/tops.12592. Navigating to goal locations in aknown environment (wayfinding) can be accomplished by different strategies, notably by taking habitual, well-learned routes (response strategy) or by inferring novel paths, such as shortcuts, from spatial knowledge of the environment'slayout (place strategy).
